title: CloudEvent
description: BEST envelope for commands and events — a conformant profile of the CloudEvents 1.0 specification. BEST restricts CloudEvents (PascalCase type, JSON-only content, dataschema required for commands) but every valid BEST message is a valid CloudEvent 1.0 message. See design-decisions.md#cloudevents-conformance.

cloudEvent.description: A CloudEvent 1.0 message. Used as the wire format for BEST commands (sent to a service) and BEST events (published by a service). The data property is a domain-specific payload whose shape is defined by the JSON Schema at dataschema. Consumers MUST ignore unknown attributes (CloudEvents extension attributes and future protocol fields) rather than reject messages carrying them.

id.description: Unique message identifier. UUID recommended. Must be unique within the scope of the source.

source.description: URI-reference (RFC 3986) identifying the origin of the message. An absolute URI is recommended for interoperability; a relative reference (e.g. a service name or routing key such as 'dotquant') is valid. For commands: set by the sender. For events: set by the publishing service. Never treated as authenticated identity.

type.description: Message type identifier in PascalCase (e.g. ProposeCounter, CounterProposed). For commands this must match a type in the command catalogue. For events this identifies the domain fact.

datacontenttype.description: Content type of the data payload. Always 'application/json' in BEST (a profile restriction; CloudEvents allows any media type).

dataschema.description: Absolute URI of the JSON Schema that defines the structure of data — for commands, the catalogue entry's dataschema value, which resolves to GET /commands/{schema}/{version} on the service's own surface. Required for commands, optional for events — when omitted the consumer is responsible for interpreting the data payload (untyped event). Servers validate against their own catalogue keyed by type and never fetch this URI.

time.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the message was created.

data.description: The domain payload. For commands: validated against the schema at dataschema before queuing. For events: the recorded fact — opaque to the protocol.
